class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceACHDecline.amount
documentation: The declined amount in USD cents.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceACHDecline.inbound_ach_transfer_id
documentation: The identifier of the Inbound ACH Transfer object associated with this decline.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceACHDecline.originator_company_descriptive_date
documentation: The descriptive date of the transfer.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceACHDecline.originator_company_discretionary_data
documentation: The additional information included with the transfer.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceACHDecline.originator_company_id
documentation: The identifier of the company that initiated the transfer.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceACHDecline.originator_company_name
documentation: The name of the company that initiated the transfer.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceACHDecline.reason
documentation:
Why the ACH transfer was declined.

- `ach_route_canceled` - The account number is canceled.
- `ach_route_disabled` - The account number is disabled.
- `breaches_limit` - The transaction would cause an Increase limit to be
  exceeded.
- `entity_not_active` - The account's entity is not active.
- `group_locked` - Your account is inactive.
- `transaction_not_allowed` - The transaction is not allowed per Increase's
  terms.
- `user_initiated` - Your integration declined this transfer via the API.
- `insufficient_funds` - Your account contains insufficient funds.
- `returned_per_odfi_request` - The originating financial institution asked for
  this transfer to be returned. The receiving bank is complying with the
  request.
- `authorization_revoked_by_customer` - The customer no longer authorizes this
  transaction.
- `payment_stopped` - The customer asked for the payment to be stopped.
- `customer_advised_unauthorized_improper_ineligible_or_incomplete` - The
  customer advises that the debit was unauthorized.
- `representative_payee_deceased_or_unable_to_continue_in_that_capacity` - The
  payee is deceased.
- `beneficiary_or_account_holder_deceased` - The account holder is deceased.
- `credit_entry_refused_by_receiver` - The customer refused a credit entry.
- `duplicate_entry` - The account holder identified this transaction as a
  duplicate.
- `corporate_customer_advised_not_authorized` - The corporate customer no longer
  authorizes this transaction.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceACHDecline.receiver_id_number
documentation: The id of the receiver of the transfer.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceACHDecline.receiver_name
documentation: The name of the receiver of the transfer.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceACHDecline.trace_number
documentation: The trace number of the transfer.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceACHDecline.type
documentation:
A constant representing the object's type.

For this resource it will always be `ach_decline`.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDeclineNetworkDetailsVisa.electronic_commerce_indicator
documentation:
For electronic commerce transactions, this identifies the level of security used
in obtaining the customer's payment credential. For mail or telephone order
transactions, identifies the type of mail or telephone order.

- `mail_phone_order` - Single transaction of a mail/phone order: Use to indicate
  that the transaction is a mail/phone order purchase, not a recurring
  transaction or installment payment. For domestic transactions in the US
  region, this value may also indicate one bill payment transaction in the
  card-present or card-absent environments.
- `recurring` - Recurring transaction: Payment indicator used to indicate a
  recurring transaction that originates from an acquirer in the US region.
- `installment` - Installment payment: Payment indicator used to indicate one
  purchase of goods or services that is billed to the account in multiple
  charges over a period of time agreed upon by the cardholder and merchant from
  transactions that originate from an acquirer in the US region.
- `unknown_mail_phone_order` - Unknown classification: other mail order: Use to
  indicate that the type of mail/telephone order is unknown.
- `secure_electronic_commerce` - Secure electronic commerce transaction: Use to
  indicate that the electronic commerce transaction has been authenticated using
  e.g., 3-D Secure
- `non_authenticated_security_transaction_at_3ds_capable_merchant` -
  Non-authenticated security transaction at a 3-D Secure-capable merchant, and
  merchant attempted to authenticate the cardholder using 3-D Secure: Use to
  identify an electronic commerce transaction where the merchant attempted to
  authenticate the cardholder using 3-D Secure, but was unable to complete the
  authentication because the issuer or cardholder does not participate in the
  3-D Secure program.
- `non_authenticated_security_transaction` - Non-authenticated security
  transaction: Use to identify an electronic commerce transaction that uses data
  encryption for security however , cardholder authentication is not performed
  using 3-D Secure.
- `non_secure_transaction` - Non-secure transaction: Use to identify an
  electronic commerce transaction that has no data protection.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDeclineNetworkDetailsVisa.point_of_service_entry_mode
documentation:
The method used to enter the cardholder's primary account number and card
expiration date.

- `unknown` - Unknown
- `manual` - Manual key entry
- `magnetic_stripe_no_cvv` - Magnetic stripe read, without card verification
  value
- `optical_code` - Optical code
- `integrated_circuit_card` - Contact chip card
- `contactless` - Contactless read of chip card
- `credential_on_file` - Transaction initiated using a credential that has
  previously been stored on file
- `magnetic_stripe` - Magnetic stripe read
- `contactless_magnetic_stripe` - Contactless read of magnetic stripe data
- `integrated_circuit_card_no_cvv` - Contact chip card, without card
  verification value

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDeclineNetworkDetailsVisa.stand_in_processing_reason
documentation:
Only present when `actioner: network`.

Describes why a card authorization was approved or declined by Visa through
stand-in processing.

- `issuer_error` - Increase failed to process the authorization in a timely
  manner.
- `invalid_physical_card` - The physical card read had an invalid CVV, dCVV, or
  authorization request cryptogram.
- `invalid_cardholder_authentication_verification_value` - The 3DS cardholder
  authentication verification value was invalid.
- `internal_visa_error` - An internal Visa error occurred. Visa uses this reason
  code for certain expected occurrences as well, such as Application Transaction
  Counter (ATC) replays.
- `merchant_transaction_advisory_service_authentication_required` - The merchant
  has enabled Visa's Transaction Advisory Service and requires further
  authentication to perform the transaction. In practice this is often utilized
  at fuel pumps to tell the cardholder to see the cashier.
- `other` - An unspecific reason for stand-in processing.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDeclineNetworkDetails.category
documentation:
The payment network used to process this card authorization.

- `visa` - Visa

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDeclineNetworkDetails.visa
documentation: Fields specific to the `visa` network.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDeclineNetworkIdentifiers.retrieval_reference_number
documentation:
A life-cycle identifier used across e.g., an authorization and a reversal.

Expected to be unique per acquirer within a window of time. For some card
networks the retrieval reference number includes the trace counter.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDeclineNetworkIdentifiers.trace_number
documentation:
A counter used to verify an individual authorization.

Expected to be unique per acquirer within a window of time.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDeclineNetworkIdentifiers.transaction_id
documentation:
A globally unique transaction identifier provided by the card network, used
across multiple life-cycle requests.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDeclineVerificationCardVerificationCode.result
documentation:
The result of verifying the Card Verification Code.

- `not_checked` - No card verification code was provided in the authorization
  request.
- `match` - The card verification code matched the one on file.
- `no_match` - The card verification code did not match the one on file.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDeclineVerificationCardholderAddress.actual_line1
documentation: Line 1 of the address on file for the cardholder.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDeclineVerificationCardholderAddress.actual_postal_code
documentation: The postal code of the address on file for the cardholder.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDeclineVerificationCardholderAddress.provided_line1
documentation:
The cardholder address line 1 provided for verification in the authorization
request.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDeclineVerificationCardholderAddress.provided_postal_code
documentation: The postal code provided for verification in the authorization request.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDeclineVerificationCardholderAddress.result
documentation:
The address verification result returned to the card network.

- `not_checked` - No adress was provided in the authorization request.
- `postal_code_match_address_not_checked` - Postal code matches, but the street
  address was not verified.
- `postal_code_match_address_no_match` - Postal code matches, but the street
  address does not match.
- `postal_code_no_match_address_match` - Postal code does not match, but the
  street address matches.
- `match` - Postal code and street address match.
- `no_match` - Postal code and street address do not match.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDeclineVerification.card_verification_code
documentation:
Fields related to verification of the Card Verification Code, a 3-digit code on
the back of the card.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDeclineVerification.cardholder_address
documentation:
Cardholder address provided in the authorization request and the address on file
we verified it against.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.id
documentation: The Card Decline identifier.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.actioner
documentation:
Whether this authorization was approved by Increase, the card network through
stand-in processing, or the user through a real-time decision.

- `user` - This object was actioned by the user through a real-time decision.
- `increase` - This object was actioned by Increase without user intervention.
- `network` - This object was actioned by the network, through stand-in
  processing.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.amount
documentation:
The declined amount in the minor unit of the destination account currency.

For dollars, for example, this is cents.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.card_payment_id
documentation: The ID of the Card Payment this transaction belongs to.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.currency
documentation:
The [ISO 4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) code for the destination
account currency.

- `CAD` - Canadian Dollar (CAD)
- `CHF` - Swiss Franc (CHF)
- `EUR` - Euro (EUR)
- `GBP` - British Pound (GBP)
- `JPY` - Japanese Yen (JPY)
- `USD` - US Dollar (USD)

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.declined_transaction_id
documentation: The identifier of the declined transaction created for this Card Decline.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.digital_wallet_token_id
documentation:
If the authorization was made via a Digital Wallet Token (such as an Apple Pay
purchase), the identifier of the token that was used.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.direction
documentation:
The direction describes the direction the funds will move, either from the
cardholder to the merchant or from the merchant to the cardholder.

- `settlement` - A regular card authorization where funds are debited from the
  cardholder.
- `refund` - A refund card authorization, sometimes referred to as a credit
  voucher authorization, where funds are credited to the cardholder.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.merchant_acceptor_id
documentation:
The merchant identifier (commonly abbreviated as MID) of the merchant the card
is transacting with.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.merchant_category_code
documentation:
The Merchant Category Code (commonly abbreviated as MCC) of the merchant the
card is transacting with.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.merchant_city
documentation: The city the merchant resides in.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.merchant_country
documentation: The country the merchant resides in.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.merchant_descriptor
documentation: The merchant descriptor of the merchant the card is transacting with.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.merchant_postal_code
documentation:
The merchant's postal code.

For US merchants this is either a 5-digit or 9-digit ZIP code, where the first 5
and last 4 are separated by a dash.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.merchant_state
documentation: The state the merchant resides in.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.network_details
documentation: Fields specific to the `network`.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.network_identifiers
documentation: Network-specific identifiers for a specific request or transaction.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.network_risk_score
documentation:
The risk score generated by the card network.

For Visa this is the Visa Advanced Authorization risk score, from 0 to 99, where
99 is the riskiest.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.physical_card_id
documentation:
If the authorization was made in-person with a physical card, the Physical Card
that was used.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.presentment_amount
documentation: The declined amount in the minor unit of the transaction's presentment currency.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.presentment_currency
documentation:
The [ISO 4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) code for the
transaction's presentment currency.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.processing_category
documentation:
The processing category describes the intent behind the authorization, such as
whether it was used for bill payments or an automatic fuel dispenser.

- `account_funding` - Account funding transactions are transactions used to
  e.g., fund an account or transfer funds between accounts.
- `automatic_fuel_dispenser` - Automatic fuel dispenser authorizations occur
  when a card is used at a gas pump, prior to the actual transaction amount
  being known. They are followed by an advice message that updates the amount of
  the pending transaction.
- `bill_payment` - A transaction used to pay a bill.
- `purchase` - A regular purchase.
- `quasi_cash` - Quasi-cash transactions represent purchases of items which may
  be convertible to cash.
- `refund` - A refund card authorization, sometimes referred to as a credit
  voucher authorization, where funds are credited to the cardholder.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.real_time_decision_id
documentation:
The identifier of the Real-Time Decision sent to approve or decline this
transaction.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.real_time_decision_reason
documentation:
This is present if a specific decline reason was given in the real-time
decision.

- `insufficient_funds` - The cardholder does not have sufficient funds to cover
  the transaction. The merchant may attempt to process the transaction again.
- `transaction_never_allowed` - This type of transaction is not allowed for this
  card. This transaction should not be retried.
- `exceeds_approval_limit` - The transaction amount exceeds the cardholder's
  approval limit. The merchant may attempt to process the transaction again.
- `card_temporarily_disabled` - The card has been temporarily disabled or not
  yet activated. The merchant may attempt to process the transaction again.
- `suspected_fraud` - The transaction is suspected to be fraudulent. The
  merchant may attempt to process the transaction again.
- `other` - The transaction was declined for another reason. The merchant may
  attempt to process the transaction again. This should be used sparingly.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.reason
documentation:
Why the transaction was declined.

- `card_not_active` - The Card was not active.
- `physical_card_not_active` - The Physical Card was not active.
- `entity_not_active` - The account's entity was not active.
- `group_locked` - The account was inactive.
- `insufficient_funds` - The Card's Account did not have a sufficient available
  balance.
- `cvv2_mismatch` - The given CVV2 did not match the card's value.
- `card_expiration_mismatch` - The given expiration date did not match the
  card's value. Only applies when a CVV2 is present.
- `transaction_not_allowed` - The attempted card transaction is not allowed per
  Increase's terms.
- `breaches_limit` - The transaction was blocked by a Limit.
- `webhook_declined` - Your application declined the transaction via webhook.
- `webhook_timed_out` - Your application webhook did not respond without the
  required timeout.
- `declined_by_stand_in_processing` - Declined by stand-in processing.
- `invalid_physical_card` - The card read had an invalid CVV, dCVV, or
  authorization request cryptogram.
- `missing_original_authorization` - The original card authorization for this
  incremental authorization does not exist.
- `suspected_fraud` - The transaction was suspected to be fraudulent. Please
  reach out to support@increase.com for more information.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.terminal_id
documentation:
The terminal identifier (commonly abbreviated as TID) of the terminal the card
is transacting with.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCardDecline.verification
documentation: Fields related to verification of cardholder-provided values.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCheckDecline.amount
documentation: The declined amount in USD cents.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCheckDecline.auxiliary_on_us
documentation:
A computer-readable number printed on the MICR line of business checks, usually
the check number. This is useful for positive pay checks, but can be unreliably
transmitted by the bank of first deposit.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCheckDecline.back_image_file_id
documentation:
The identifier of the API File object containing an image of the back of the
declined check.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCheckDecline.check_transfer_id
documentation: The identifier of the Check Transfer object associated with this decline.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCheckDecline.front_image_file_id
documentation:
The identifier of the API File object containing an image of the front of the
declined check.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCheckDecline.inbound_check_deposit_id
documentation: The identifier of the Inbound Check Deposit object associated with this decline.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCheckDecline.reason
documentation:
Why the check was declined.

- `ach_route_disabled` - The account number is disabled.
- `ach_route_canceled` - The account number is canceled.
- `altered_or_fictitious` - The deposited check was altered or fictitious.
- `breaches_limit` - The transaction would cause a limit to be exceeded.
- `endorsement_irregular` - The check was not endorsed by the payee.
- `entity_not_active` - The account's entity is not active.
- `group_locked` - Your account is inactive.
- `insufficient_funds` - Your account contains insufficient funds.
- `stop_payment_requested` - Stop payment requested for this check.
- `duplicate_presentment` - The check was a duplicate deposit.
- `not_authorized` - The check was not authorized.
- `amount_mismatch` - The amount the receiving bank is attempting to deposit
  does not match the amount on the check.
- `not_our_item` - The check attempting to be deposited does not belong to
  Increase.
- `no_account_number_found` - The account number on the check does not exist at
  Increase.
- `refer_to_image` - The check is not readable. Please refer to the image.
- `unable_to_process` - The check cannot be processed. This is rare: please
  contact support.
- `user_initiated` - Your integration declined this check via the API.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCheckDepositRejection.amount
documentation:
The rejected amount in the minor unit of check's currency.

For dollars, for example, this is cents.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCheckDepositRejection.check_deposit_id
documentation: The identifier of the Check Deposit that was rejected.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCheckDepositRejection.currency
documentation:
The [ISO 4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) code for the check's
currency.

- `CAD` - Canadian Dollar (CAD)
- `CHF` - Swiss Franc (CHF)
- `EUR` - Euro (EUR)
- `GBP` - British Pound (GBP)
- `JPY` - Japanese Yen (JPY)
- `USD` - US Dollar (USD)

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCheckDepositRejection.declined_transaction_id
documentation: The identifier of the associated declined transaction.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCheckDepositRejection.reason
documentation:
Why the check deposit was rejected.

- `incomplete_image` - The check's image is incomplete.
- `duplicate` - This is a duplicate check submission.
- `poor_image_quality` - This check has poor image quality.
- `incorrect_amount` - The check was deposited with the incorrect amount.
- `incorrect_recipient` - The check is made out to someone other than the
  account holder.
- `not_eligible_for_mobile_deposit` - This check was not eligible for mobile
  deposit.
- `missing_required_data_elements` - This check is missing at least one required
  field.
- `suspected_fraud` - This check is suspected to be fraudulent.
- `deposit_window_expired` - This check's deposit window has expired.
- `requested_by_user` - The check was rejected at the user's request.
- `unknown` - The check was rejected for an unknown reason.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceCheckDepositRejection.rejected_at
documentation:
The [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) date and time at which
the check deposit was rejected.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceInboundRealTimePaymentsTransferDecline.amount
documentation:
The declined amount in the minor unit of the destination account currency.

For dollars, for example, this is cents.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceInboundRealTimePaymentsTransferDecline.creditor_name
documentation: The name the sender of the transfer specified as the recipient of the transfer.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceInboundRealTimePaymentsTransferDecline.currency
documentation:
The [ISO 4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) code of the declined
transfer's currency. This will always be "USD" for a Real-Time Payments
transfer.

- `CAD` - Canadian Dollar (CAD)
- `CHF` - Swiss Franc (CHF)
- `EUR` - Euro (EUR)
- `GBP` - British Pound (GBP)
- `JPY` - Japanese Yen (JPY)
- `USD` - US Dollar (USD)

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceInboundRealTimePaymentsTransferDecline.debtor_account_number
documentation: The account number of the account that sent the transfer.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceInboundRealTimePaymentsTransferDecline.debtor_name
documentation: The name provided by the sender of the transfer.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceInboundRealTimePaymentsTransferDecline.debtor_routing_number
documentation: The routing number of the account that sent the transfer.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceInboundRealTimePaymentsTransferDecline.reason
documentation:
Why the transfer was declined.

- `account_number_canceled` - The account number is canceled.
- `account_number_disabled` - The account number is disabled.
- `account_restricted` - Your account is restricted.
- `group_locked` - Your account is inactive.
- `entity_not_active` - The account's entity is not active.
- `real_time_payments_not_enabled` - Your account is not enabled to receive
  Real-Time Payments transfers.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceInboundRealTimePaymentsTransferDecline.remittance_information
documentation: Additional information included with the transfer.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceInboundRealTimePaymentsTransferDecline.transaction_identification
documentation: The Real-Time Payments network identification of the declined transfer.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceInboundRealTimePaymentsTransferDecline.transfer_id
documentation: The identifier of the Real-Time Payments Transfer that led to this Transaction.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceWireDecline.inbound_wire_transfer_id
documentation: The identifier of the Inbound Wire Transfer that was declined.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.SourceWireDecline.reason
documentation:
Why the wire transfer was declined.

- `account_number_canceled` - The account number is canceled.
- `account_number_disabled` - The account number is disabled.
- `entity_not_active` - The account's entity is not active.
- `group_locked` - Your account is inactive.
- `no_account_number` - The beneficiary account number does not exist.
- `transaction_not_allowed` - The transaction is not allowed per Increase's
  terms.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.Source.ach_decline
documentation:
An ACH Decline object.

This field will be present in the JSON response if and only if `category` is
equal to `ach_decline`.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.Source.card_decline
documentation:
A Card Decline object.

This field will be present in the JSON response if and only if `category` is
equal to `card_decline`.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.Source.category
documentation:
The type of the resource.

We may add additional possible values for this enum over time; your application
should be able to handle such additions gracefully.

- `ach_decline` - ACH Decline: details will be under the `ach_decline` object.
- `card_decline` - Card Decline: details will be under the `card_decline`
  object.
- `check_decline` - Check Decline: details will be under the `check_decline`
  object.
- `inbound_real_time_payments_transfer_decline` - Inbound Real-Time Payments
  Transfer Decline: details will be under the
  `inbound_real_time_payments_transfer_decline` object.
- `wire_decline` - Wire Decline: details will be under the `wire_decline`
  object.
- `check_deposit_rejection` - Check Deposit Rejection: details will be under the
  `check_deposit_rejection` object.
- `other` - The Declined Transaction was made for an undocumented or deprecated
  reason.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.Source.check_decline
documentation:
A Check Decline object.

This field will be present in the JSON response if and only if `category` is
equal to `check_decline`.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.Source.check_deposit_rejection
documentation:
A Check Deposit Rejection object.

This field will be present in the JSON response if and only if `category` is
equal to `check_deposit_rejection`.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.Source.inbound_real_time_payments_transfer_decline
documentation:
An Inbound Real-Time Payments Transfer Decline object.

This field will be present in the JSON response if and only if `category` is
equal to `inbound_real_time_payments_transfer_decline`.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.Source.other
documentation:
If the category of this Transaction source is equal to `other`, this field will
contain an empty object, otherwise it will contain null.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.Source.wire_decline
documentation:
A Wire Decline object.

This field will be present in the JSON response if and only if `category` is
equal to `wire_decline`.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.DeclinedTransaction.id
documentation: The Declined Transaction identifier.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.DeclinedTransaction.account_id
documentation: The identifier for the Account the Declined Transaction belongs to.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.DeclinedTransaction.amount
documentation:
The Declined Transaction amount in the minor unit of its currency.

For dollars, for example, this is cents.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.DeclinedTransaction.created_at
documentation:
The [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) date on which the
Transaction occurred.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.DeclinedTransaction.currency
documentation:
The [ISO 4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) code for the Declined
Transaction's currency. This will match the currency on the Declined
Transaction's Account.

- `CAD` - Canadian Dollar (CAD)
- `CHF` - Swiss Franc (CHF)
- `EUR` - Euro (EUR)
- `GBP` - British Pound (GBP)
- `JPY` - Japanese Yen (JPY)
- `USD` - US Dollar (USD)

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.DeclinedTransaction.description
documentation: This is the description the vendor provides.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.DeclinedTransaction.route_id
documentation:
The identifier for the route this Declined Transaction came through.

Routes are things like cards and ACH details.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.DeclinedTransaction.route_type
documentation:
The type of the route this Declined Transaction came through.

- `account_number` - An Account Number.
- `card` - A Card.
- `lockbox` - A Lockbox.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.DeclinedTransaction.source
documentation:
This is an object giving more details on the network-level event that caused the
Declined Transaction. For example, for a card transaction this lists the
merchant's industry and location. Note that for backwards compatibility reasons,
additional undocumented keys may appear in this object. These should be treated
as deprecated and will be removed in the future.

class_variables.name: increase.types.declined_transaction.DeclinedTransaction.type
documentation:
A constant representing the object's type.

For this resource it will always be `declined_transaction`.

class_variables.name: increase.types.entity_create_beneficial_owner_params.BeneficialOwnerIndividualIdentification.passport

tax id (either a Social Security Number or Individual Taxpayer Identification
Number).

submit an array containing both.

class_variables.name: increase.types.entity_create_beneficial_owner_params.BeneficialOwner.company_title

documentation: File generated from our OpenAPI spec by Stainless. See CONTRIBUTING.md for details.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.Adjustment.adjusted_at
documentation: The time at which the return adjustment was received.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.Adjustment.amount
documentation: The amount of the adjustment.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.Adjustment.reason
documentation:
The reason for the adjustment.

- `late_return` - The return was initiated too late and the receiving
  institution has responded with a Late Return Claim.
- `wrong_payee_credit` - The check was deposited to the wrong payee and the
  depositing institution has reimbursed the funds with a Wrong Payee Credit.
- `adjusted_amount` - The check was deposited with a different amount than what
  was written on the check.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.Adjustment.transaction_id
documentation: The id of the transaction for the adjustment.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.DepositReturn.reason
documentation:
The reason the deposit was returned.

- `altered_or_fictitious` - The check was altered or fictitious.
- `not_authorized` - The check was not authorized.
- `duplicate_presentment` - The check was a duplicate presentment.
- `endorsement_missing` - The check was not endorsed.
- `endorsement_irregular` - The check was not endorsed by the payee.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.DepositReturn.returned_at
documentation: The time at which the deposit was returned.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.DepositReturn.transaction_id
documentation: The id of the transaction for the returned deposit.

documentation: The deposit's identifier.

If the Inbound Check Deposit was accepted, the
[ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) date and time at which this
took place.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.InboundCheckDeposit.account_id
documentation: The Account the check is being deposited against.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.InboundCheckDeposit.account_number_id
documentation: The Account Number the check is being deposited against.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.InboundCheckDeposit.adjustments
documentation:
If the deposit or the return was adjusted by the sending institution, this will
contain details of the adjustments.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.InboundCheckDeposit.amount
documentation: The deposited amount in USD cents.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.InboundCheckDeposit.back_image_file_id
documentation: The ID for the File containing the image of the back of the check.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.InboundCheckDeposit.bank_of_first_deposit_routing_number
documentation:
The American Bankers' Association (ABA) Routing Transit Number (RTN) for the
bank depositing this check. In some rare cases, this is not transmitted via
Check21 and the value will be null.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.InboundCheckDeposit.check_number
documentation: The check number printed on the check being deposited.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.InboundCheckDeposit.check_transfer_id
documentation:
If this deposit is for an existing Check Transfer, the identifier of that Check
Transfer.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.InboundCheckDeposit.created_at
documentation:
The [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) date and time at which
the deposit was attempted.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.InboundCheckDeposit.currency
documentation:
The [ISO 4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) code for the deposit.

- `CAD` - Canadian Dollar (CAD)
- `CHF` - Swiss Franc (CHF)
- `EUR` - Euro (EUR)
- `GBP` - British Pound (GBP)
- `JPY` - Japanese Yen (JPY)
- `USD` - US Dollar (USD)

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.InboundCheckDeposit.declined_at
documentation:
If the Inbound Check Deposit was declined, the
[ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) date and time at which this
took place.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.InboundCheckDeposit.declined_transaction_id
documentation:
If the deposit attempt has been rejected, the identifier of the Declined
Transaction object created as a result of the failed deposit.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.InboundCheckDeposit.deposit_return
documentation:
If you requested a return of this deposit, this will contain details of the
return.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.InboundCheckDeposit.front_image_file_id
documentation: The ID for the File containing the image of the front of the check.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.InboundCheckDeposit.payee_name_analysis
documentation:
Whether the details on the check match the recipient name of the check transfer.

This is an optional feature, contact sales to enable.

- `name_matches` - The details on the check match the recipient name of the
  check transfer.
- `does_not_match` - The details on the check do not match the recipient name of
  the check transfer.
- `not_evaluated` - The payee name analysis was not evaluated.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.InboundCheckDeposit.status
documentation:
The status of the Inbound Check Deposit.

- `pending` - The Inbound Check Deposit is pending.
- `accepted` - The Inbound Check Deposit was accepted.
- `declined` - The Inbound Check Deposit was rejected.
- `returned` - The Inbound Check Deposit was returned.
- `requires_attention` - The Inbound Check Deposit requires attention from an
  Increase operator.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.InboundCheckDeposit.transaction_id
documentation:
If the deposit attempt has been accepted, the identifier of the Transaction
object created as a result of the successful deposit.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_check_deposit.InboundCheckDeposit.type
documentation:
A constant representing the object's type.

For this resource it will always be `inbound_check_deposit`.

documentation:

  transactions and balances for a given time range and Account.
- `transaction_csv` - Export a CSV of all transactions for a given time range.
- `balance_csv` - Export a CSV of account balances for the dates in a given
  range.
- `bookkeeping_account_balance_csv` - Export a CSV of bookkeeping account
  balances for the dates in a given range.
- `entity_csv` - Export a CSV of entities with a given status.
- `vendor_csv` - Export a CSV of vendors added to the third-party risk
  management dashboard.

modules.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer
documentation: File generated from our OpenAPI spec by Stainless. See CONTRIBUTING.md for details.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.Acceptance.accepted_at
documentation: The time at which the transfer was accepted.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.Acceptance.transaction_id
documentation: The id of the transaction for the accepted transfer.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.AddendaFreeformEntry.payment_related_information
documentation: The payment related information passed in the addendum.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.AddendaFreeform.entries
documentation: Each entry represents an addendum received from the originator.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.Addenda.category
documentation:
The type of addendum.

- `freeform` - Unstructured addendum.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.Addenda.freeform
documentation: Unstructured `payment_related_information` passed through by the originator.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.Decline.declined_at
documentation: The time at which the transfer was declined.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.Decline.declined_transaction_id
documentation: The id of the transaction for the declined transfer.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.Decline.reason
documentation:
The reason for the transfer decline.

- `ach_route_canceled` - The account number is canceled.
- `ach_route_disabled` - The account number is disabled.
- `breaches_limit` - The transaction would cause an Increase limit to be
  exceeded.
- `entity_not_active` - The account's entity is not active.
- `group_locked` - Your account is inactive.
- `transaction_not_allowed` - The transaction is not allowed per Increase's
  terms.
- `user_initiated` - Your integration declined this transfer via the API.
- `insufficient_funds` - Your account contains insufficient funds.
- `returned_per_odfi_request` - The originating financial institution asked for
  this transfer to be returned. The receiving bank is complying with the
  request.
- `authorization_revoked_by_customer` - The customer no longer authorizes this
  transaction.
- `payment_stopped` - The customer asked for the payment to be stopped.
- `customer_advised_unauthorized_improper_ineligible_or_incomplete` - The
  customer advises that the debit was unauthorized.
- `representative_payee_deceased_or_unable_to_continue_in_that_capacity` - The
  payee is deceased.
- `beneficiary_or_account_holder_deceased` - The account holder is deceased.
- `credit_entry_refused_by_receiver` - The customer refused a credit entry.
- `duplicate_entry` - The account holder identified this transaction as a
  duplicate.
- `corporate_customer_advised_not_authorized` - The corporate customer no longer
  authorizes this transaction.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.InternationalAddenda.destination_country_code
documentation:
The [ISO 3166](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2), Alpha-2
country code of the destination country.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.InternationalAddenda.destination_currency_code
documentation:
The [ISO 4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) currency code for the
destination bank account.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.InternationalAddenda.foreign_exchange_indicator
documentation:
A description of how the foreign exchange rate was calculated.

- `fixed_to_variable` - The originator chose an amount in their own currency.
  The settled amount in USD was converted using the exchange rate.
- `variable_to_fixed` - The originator chose an amount to settle in USD. The
  originator's amount was variable; known only after the foreign exchange
  conversion.
- `fixed_to_fixed` - The amount was originated and settled as a fixed amount in
  USD. There is no foreign exchange conversion.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.InternationalAddenda.foreign_exchange_reference
documentation:
Depending on the `foreign_exchange_reference_indicator`, an exchange rate or a
reference to a well-known rate.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.InternationalAddenda.foreign_exchange_reference_indicator
documentation:
An instruction of how to interpret the `foreign_exchange_reference` field for
this Transaction.

- `foreign_exchange_rate` - The ACH file contains a foreign exchange rate.
- `foreign_exchange_reference_number` - The ACH file contains a reference to a
  well-known foreign exchange rate.
- `blank` - There is no foreign exchange for this transfer, so the
  `foreign_exchange_reference` field is blank.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.InternationalAddenda.foreign_payment_amount
documentation:
The amount in the minor unit of the foreign payment currency.

For dollars, for example, this is cents.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.InternationalAddenda.foreign_trace_number
documentation: A reference number in the foreign banking infrastructure.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.InternationalAddenda.international_transaction_type_code
documentation:
The type of transfer. Set by the originator.

- `annuity` - Sent as `ANN` in the Nacha file.
- `business_or_commercial` - Sent as `BUS` in the Nacha file.
- `deposit` - Sent as `DEP` in the Nacha file.
- `loan` - Sent as `LOA` in the Nacha file.
- `miscellaneous` - Sent as `MIS` in the Nacha file.
- `mortgage` - Sent as `MOR` in the Nacha file.
- `pension` - Sent as `PEN` in the Nacha file.
- `remittance` - Sent as `REM` in the Nacha file.
- `rent_or_lease` - Sent as `RLS` in the Nacha file.
- `salary_or_payroll` - Sent as `SAL` in the Nacha file.
- `tax` - Sent as `TAX` in the Nacha file.
- `accounts_receivable` - Sent as `ARC` in the Nacha file.
- `back_office_conversion` - Sent as `BOC` in the Nacha file.
- `machine_transfer` - Sent as `MTE` in the Nacha file.
- `point_of_purchase` - Sent as `POP` in the Nacha file.
- `point_of_sale` - Sent as `POS` in the Nacha file.
- `represented_check` - Sent as `RCK` in the Nacha file.
- `shared_network_transaction` - Sent as `SHR` in the Nacha file.
- `telphone_initiated` - Sent as `TEL` in the Nacha file.
- `internet_initiated` - Sent as `WEB` in the Nacha file.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.InternationalAddenda.originating_currency_code
documentation:
The [ISO 4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) currency code for the
originating bank account.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.InternationalAddenda.originating_depository_financial_institution_branch_country
documentation:
The [ISO 3166](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2), Alpha-2
country code of the originating branch country.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.InternationalAddenda.originating_depository_financial_institution_id
documentation:
An identifier for the originating bank.

One of an International Bank Account Number (IBAN) bank identifier, SWIFT Bank
Identification Code (BIC), or a domestic identifier like a US Routing Number.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.TransferReturn.reason
documentation:
The reason for the transfer return.

- `insufficient_funds` - The customer's account has insufficient funds. This
  reason is only allowed for debits. The Nacha return code is R01.
- `returned_per_odfi_request` - The originating financial institution asked for
  this transfer to be returned. The receiving bank is complying with the
  request. The Nacha return code is R06.
- `authorization_revoked_by_customer` - The customer no longer authorizes this
  transaction. The Nacha return code is R07.
- `payment_stopped` - The customer asked for the payment to be stopped. This
  reason is only allowed for debits. The Nacha return code is R08.
- `customer_advised_unauthorized_improper_ineligible_or_incomplete` - The
  customer advises that the debit was unauthorized. The Nacha return code is
  R10.
- `representative_payee_deceased_or_unable_to_continue_in_that_capacity` - The
  payee is deceased. The Nacha return code is R14.
- `beneficiary_or_account_holder_deceased` - The account holder is deceased. The
  Nacha return code is R15.
- `credit_entry_refused_by_receiver` - The customer refused a credit entry. This
  reason is only allowed for credits. The Nacha return code is R23.
- `duplicate_entry` - The account holder identified this transaction as a
  duplicate. The Nacha return code is R24.
- `corporate_customer_advised_not_authorized` - The corporate customer no longer
  authorizes this transaction. The Nacha return code is R29.

- `future_dated` - The transfer is expected to settle on a future date.

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.InboundACHTransfer.standard_entry_class_code
documentation:
The Standard Entry Class (SEC) code of the transfer.

- `corporate_credit_or_debit` - Corporate Credit and Debit (CCD).
- `corporate_trade_exchange` - Corporate Trade Exchange (CTX).
- `prearranged_payments_and_deposit` - Prearranged Payments and Deposits (PPD).
- `internet_initiated` - Internet Initiated (WEB).
- `point_of_sale` - Point of Sale (POS).
- `telephone_initiated` - Telephone Initiated (TEL).
- `customer_initiated` - Customer Initiated (CIE).
- `accounts_receivable` - Accounts Receivable (ARC).
- `machine_transfer` - Machine Transfer (MTE).
- `shared_network_transaction` - Shared Network Transaction (SHR).
- `represented_check` - Represented Check (RCK).
- `back_office_conversion` - Back Office Conversion (BOC).
- `point_of_purchase` - Point of Purchase (POP).
- `check_truncation` - Check Truncation (TRC).
- `destroyed_check` - Destroyed Check (XCK).
- `international_ach_transaction` - International ACH Transaction (IAT).

class_variables.name: increase.types.inbound_ach_transfer.InboundACHTransfer.status
documentation:
The status of the transfer.

- `pending` - The Inbound ACH Transfer is awaiting action, will transition
  automatically if no action is taken.
- `declined` - The Inbound ACH Transfer has been declined.
- `accepted` - The Inbound ACH Transfer is accepted.
- `returned` - The Inbound ACH Transfer has been returned.

For this resource it will always be `inbound_ach_transfer`.

- `disabled` - The subscription is temporarily disabled and Events will not be
  delivered.
- `deleted` - The subscription is permanently disabled and Events will not be
  delivered.
- `requires_attention` - The subscription is temporarily disabled due to
  delivery errors and Events will not be delivered.

The [ISO 4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) code for the transfer's
currency. For wire transfers this is always equal to `usd`.

- `CAD` - Canadian Dollar (CAD)
- `CHF` - Swiss Franc (CHF)
- `EUR` - Euro (EUR)
- `GBP` - British Pound (GBP)
- `JPY` - Japanese Yen (JPY)
- `USD` - US Dollar (USD)

The ID for the pending transaction representing the transfer.

A pending transaction is created when the transfer
[requires approval](https://increase.com/documentation/transfer-approvals#transfer-approvals)
by someone else in your organization.

class_variables.name: increase.types.wire_transfer.WireTransfer.reversal
documentation: If your transfer is reversed, this will contain details of the reversal.

class_variables.name: increase.types.wire_transfer.WireTransfer.routing_number
documentation: The American Bankers' Association (ABA) Routing Transit Number (RTN).

class_variables.name: increase.types.wire_transfer.WireTransfer.source_account_number_id
documentation: The Account Number that was passed to the wire's recipient.

class_variables.name: increase.types.wire_transfer.WireTransfer.status
documentation:
The lifecycle status of the transfer.

- `pending_approval` - The transfer is pending approval.
- `canceled` - The transfer has been canceled.
- `pending_reviewing` - The transfer is pending review by Increase.
- `rejected` - The transfer has been rejected by Increase.
- `requires_attention` - The transfer requires attention from an Increase
  operator.
- `pending_creating` - The transfer is pending creation.
- `reversed` - The transfer has been reversed.
- `submitted` - The transfer has been submitted to Fedwire.
- `complete` - The transfer has been acknowledged by Fedwire and can be
  considered complete.

class_variables.name: increase.types.wire_transfer.WireTransfer.submission
documentation:
After the transfer is submitted to Fedwire, this will contain supplemental
details.

class_variables.name: increase.types.wire_transfer.WireTransfer.transaction_id
documentation: The ID for the transaction funding the transfer.

class_variables.name: increase.types.wire_transfer.WireTransfer.type
documentation:
A constant representing the object's type.

For this resource it will always be `wire_transfer`.

documentation:

documentation:
If the Real-Time Decision relates to a digital wallet token provisioning
attempt, this object contains your response to the attempt.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_decision_action_params.CardAuthentication.decision
documentation:
Whether the card authentication attempt should be approved or declined.

- `approve` - Approve the authentication attempt without triggering a challenge.
- `challenge` - Request further validation before approving the authentication
  attempt.
- `deny` - Deny the authentication attempt.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_decision_action_params.CardAuthenticationChallenge.result
documentation:
Whether the card authentication challenge was successfully delivered to the
cardholder.

- `success` - Your application successfully delivered the one-time code to the
  cardholder.
- `failure` - Your application was unable to deliver the one-time code to the
  cardholder.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_decision_action_params.CardAuthorization.decision
documentation:
Whether the card authorization should be approved or declined.

- `approve` - Approve the authorization.
- `decline` - Decline the authorization.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_decision_action_params.CardAuthorization.decline_reason
documentation:
The reason the card authorization was declined.

This translates to a specific decline code that is sent to the card network.

- `insufficient_funds` - The cardholder does not have sufficient funds to cover
  the transaction. The merchant may attempt to process the transaction again.
- `transaction_never_allowed` - This type of transaction is not allowed for this
  card. This transaction should not be retried.
- `exceeds_approval_limit` - The transaction amount exceeds the cardholder's
  approval limit. The merchant may attempt to process the transaction again.
- `card_temporarily_disabled` - The card has been temporarily disabled or not
  yet activated. The merchant may attempt to process the transaction again.
- `suspected_fraud` - The transaction is suspected to be fraudulent. The
  merchant may attempt to process the transaction again.
- `other` - The transaction was declined for another reason. The merchant may
  attempt to process the transaction again. This should be used sparingly.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_decision_action_params.DigitalWalletAuthenticationSuccess.email
documentation: The email address that was used to verify the cardholder via one-time passcode.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_decision_action_params.DigitalWalletAuthenticationSuccess.phone
documentation:
The phone number that was used to verify the cardholder via one-time passcode
over SMS.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_decision_action_params.DigitalWalletAuthentication.result
documentation:
Whether your application was able to deliver the one-time passcode.

- `success` - Your application successfully delivered the one-time passcode to
  the cardholder.
- `failure` - Your application failed to deliver the one-time passcode to the
  cardholder.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_decision_action_params.DigitalWalletTokenApproval.email
documentation:
An email address that can be used to verify the cardholder via one-time
passcode.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_decision_action_params.DigitalWalletTokenApproval.phone
documentation:

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_decision_action_params.DigitalWalletTokenDecline.reason

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_decision_action_params.DigitalWalletToken.decline

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_transfer.Rejection.reject_reason_additional_information
documentation:
Additional information about the rejection provided by the recipient bank when
the `reject_reason_code` is `NARRATIVE`.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_transfer.Rejection.reject_reason_code
documentation:
The reason the transfer was rejected as provided by the recipient bank or the
Real-Time Payments network.

- `account_closed` - The destination account is closed. Corresponds to the
  Real-Time Payments reason code `AC04`.
- `account_blocked` - The destination account is currently blocked from
  receiving transactions. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code
  `AC06`.
- `invalid_creditor_account_type` - The destination account is ineligible to
  receive Real-Time Payments transfers. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments
  reason code `AC14`.
- `invalid_creditor_account_number` - The destination account does not exist.
  Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `AC03`.
- `invalid_creditor_financial_institution_identifier` - The destination routing
  number is invalid. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `RC04`.
- `end_customer_deceased` - The destination account holder is deceased.
  Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `MD07`.
- `narrative` - The reason is provided as narrative information in the
  additional information field.
- `transaction_forbidden` - Real-Time Payments transfers are not allowed to the
  destination account. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `AG01`.
- `transaction_type_not_supported` - Real-Time Payments transfers are not
  enabled for the destination account. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments
  reason code `AG03`.
- `unexpected_amount` - The amount of the transfer is different than expected by
  the recipient. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `AM09`.
- `amount_exceeds_bank_limits` - The amount is higher than the recipient is
  authorized to send or receive. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason
  code `AM14`.
- `invalid_creditor_address` - The creditor's address is required, but missing
  or invalid. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `BE04`.
- `unknown_end_customer` - The specified creditor is unknown. Corresponds to the
  Real-Time Payments reason code `BE06`.
- `invalid_debtor_address` - The debtor's address is required, but missing or
  invalid. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `BE07`.
- `timeout` - There was a timeout processing the transfer. Corresponds to the
  Real-Time Payments reason code `DS24`.
- `unsupported_message_for_recipient` - Real-Time Payments transfers are not
  enabled for the destination account. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments
  reason code `NOAT`.
- `recipient_connection_not_available` - The destination financial institution
  is currently not connected to Real-Time Payments. Corresponds to the Real-Time
  Payments reason code `9912`.
- `real_time_payments_suspended` - Real-Time Payments is currently unavailable.
  Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `9948`.
- `instructed_agent_signed_off` - The destination financial institution is
  currently signed off of Real-Time Payments. Corresponds to the Real-Time
  Payments reason code `9910`.
- `processing_error` - The transfer was rejected due to an internal Increase
  issue. We have been notified.
- `other` - Some other error or issue has occurred.

This is set by the sender when creating the transfer.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_transfer.RealTimePaymentsTransfer.currency
documentation:
The [ISO 4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) code for the transfer's
currency. For real-time payments transfers this is always equal to `USD`.

- `CAD` - Canadian Dollar (CAD)
- `CHF` - Swiss Franc (CHF)
- `EUR` - Euro (EUR)
- `GBP` - British Pound (GBP)
- `JPY` - Japanese Yen (JPY)
- `USD` - US Dollar (USD)

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_transfer.RealTimePaymentsTransfer.debtor_name
documentation:
The name of the transfer's sender.

If not provided, defaults to the name of the account's entity.

documentation: The destination account number.

documentation:
The destination American Bankers' Association (ABA) Routing Transit Number
(RTN).

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_transfer.RealTimePaymentsTransfer.external_account_id
documentation: The identifier of the External Account the transfer was made to, if any.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_transfer.RealTimePaymentsTransfer.idempotency_key
documentation:
The idempotency key you chose for this object.

This value is unique across Increase and is used to ensure that a request is
only processed once. Learn more about
[idempotency](https://increase.com/documentation/idempotency-keys).

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_transfer.RealTimePaymentsTransfer.pending_transaction_id
documentation:
The ID for the pending transaction representing the transfer.

A pending transaction is created when the transfer
[requires approval](https://increase.com/documentation/transfer-approvals#transfer-approvals)
by someone else in your organization.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_transfer.RealTimePaymentsTransfer.rejection
documentation:
If the transfer is rejected by Real-Time Payments or the destination financial
institution, this will contain supplemental details.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_transfer.RealTimePaymentsTransfer.remittance_information
documentation: Unstructured information that will show on the recipient's bank statement.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_transfer.RealTimePaymentsTransfer.source_account_number_id
documentation: The Account Number the recipient will see as having sent the transfer.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_transfer.RealTimePaymentsTransfer.status
documentation:
The lifecycle status of the transfer.

- `pending_approval` - The transfer is pending approval.
- `canceled` - The transfer has been canceled.
- `pending_reviewing` - The transfer is pending review by Increase.
- `requires_attention` - The transfer requires attention from an Increase
  operator.
- `rejected` - The transfer was rejected by the network or the recipient's bank.
- `pending_submission` - The transfer is queued to be submitted to Real-Time
  Payments.
- `submitted` - The transfer has been submitted and is pending a response from
  Real-Time Payments.
- `complete` - The transfer has been sent successfully and is complete.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_transfer.RealTimePaymentsTransfer.submission
documentation:
After the transfer is submitted to Real-Time Payments, this will contain
supplemental details.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_transfer.RealTimePaymentsTransfer.transaction_id
documentation: The Transaction funding the transfer once it is complete.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_transfer.RealTimePaymentsTransfer.type
documentation:
A constant representing the object's type.

For this resource it will always be `real_time_payments_transfer`.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_transfer.RealTimePaymentsTransfer.ultimate_creditor_name
documentation:
The name of the ultimate recipient of the transfer.

Set this if the creditor is an intermediary receiving the payment for someone
else.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_transfer.RealTimePaymentsTransfer.ultimate_debtor_name
documentation:
The name of the ultimate sender of the transfer.

Set this if the funds are being sent on behalf of someone who is not the account
holder at Increase.

class_variables.name: increase.types.account_number_update_params.AccountNumberUpdateParams.inbound_ach
documentation: Options related to how this Account Number handles inbound ACH transfers.

documentation:
Options related to how this Account Number should handle inbound check
withdrawals.

class_variables.name: increase.types.account_number_update_params.AccountNumberUpdateParams.name
documentation: The name you choose for the Account Number.

class_variables.name: increase.types.account_number_update_params.AccountNumberUpdateParams.status
documentation:
This indicates if transfers can be made to the Account Number.

- `active` - The account number is active.
- `disabled` - The account number is temporarily disabled.
- `canceled` - The account number is permanently disabled.

class_variables.name: increase.types.account_number_update_params.InboundACH.debit_status
documentation:
Whether ACH debits are allowed against this Account Number.

Note that ACH debits will be declined if this is `allowed` but the Account
Number is not active.

- `allowed` - ACH Debits are allowed.
- `blocked` - ACH Debits are blocked.

class_variables.name: increase.types.account_number_update_params.InboundChecks.status
documentation:
How Increase should process checks with this account number printed on them.

- `allowed` - Checks with this Account Number will be processed even if they are
  not associated with a Check Transfer.
- `check_transfers_only` - Checks with this Account Number will be processed
  only if they can be matched to an existing Check Transfer.

class_variables.name: increase.types.intrafi_account_enrollment.IntrafiAccountEnrollment.id
documentation: The identifier of this enrollment at IntraFi.

class_variables.name: increase.types.intrafi_account_enrollment.IntrafiAccountEnrollment.account_id
documentation: The identifier of the Increase Account being swept into the network.

class_variables.name: increase.types.intrafi_account_enrollment.IntrafiAccountEnrollment.idempotency_key

[idempotency](https://increase.com/documentation/idempotency-keys).

class_variables.name: increase.types.intrafi_account_enrollment.IntrafiAccountEnrollment.intrafi_id
documentation:
The identifier of the account in IntraFi's system.

This identifier will be printed on any IntraFi statements or documents.

class_variables.name: increase.types.intrafi_account_enrollment.IntrafiAccountEnrollment.status
documentation:
The status of the account in the network.

An account takes about one business day to go from `pending_enrolling` to
`enrolled`.

- `pending_enrolling` - The account is being added to the IntraFi network.
- `enrolled` - The account has been enrolled with IntraFi.
- `pending_unenrolling` - The account is being unenrolled from IntraFi's deposit
  sweep.
- `unenrolled` - The account was once enrolled, but is no longer enrolled at
  IntraFi.
- `requires_attention` - Something unexpected happened with this account.
  Contact Increase support.

class_variables.name: increase.types.intrafi_account_enrollment.IntrafiAccountEnrollment.type
documentation:
A constant representing the object's type.

For this resource it will always be `intrafi_account_enrollment`.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_request_for_payment.Refusal.refusal_reason_code
documentation:
The reason the request for payment was refused as provided by the recipient bank
or the customer.

- `account_blocked` - The destination account is currently blocked from
  receiving transactions. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code
  `AC06`.
- `transaction_forbidden` - Real-Time Payments transfers are not allowed to the
  destination account. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `AG01`.
- `transaction_type_not_supported` - Real-Time Payments transfers are not
  enabled for the destination account. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments
  reason code `AG03`.
- `unexpected_amount` - The amount of the transfer is different than expected by
  the recipient. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `AM09`.
- `amount_exceeds_bank_limits` - The amount is higher than the recipient is
  authorized to send or receive. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason
  code `AM14`.
- `invalid_debtor_address` - The debtor's address is required, but missing or
  invalid. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `BE07`.
- `invalid_creditor_address` - The creditor's address is required, but missing
  or invalid. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `BE04`.
- `creditor_identifier_incorrect` - Creditor identifier incorrect. Corresponds
  to the Real-Time Payments reason code `CH11`.
- `requested_by_customer` - The customer refused the request. Corresponds to the
  Real-Time Payments reason code `CUST`.
- `order_rejected` - The order was rejected. Corresponds to the Real-Time
  Payments reason code `DS04`.
- `end_customer_deceased` - The destination account holder is deceased.
  Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `MD07`.
- `customer_has_opted_out` - The customer has opted out of receiving requests
  for payments from this creditor. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason
  code `SL12`.
- `other` - Some other error or issue has occurred.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_request_for_payment.Rejection.reject_reason_code
documentation:
The reason the request for payment was rejected as provided by the recipient
bank or the Real-Time Payments network.

- `account_closed` - The destination account is closed. Corresponds to the
  Real-Time Payments reason code `AC04`.
- `account_blocked` - The destination account is currently blocked from
  receiving transactions. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code
  `AC06`.
- `invalid_creditor_account_type` - The destination account is ineligible to
  receive Real-Time Payments transfers. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments
  reason code `AC14`.
- `invalid_creditor_account_number` - The destination account does not exist.
  Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `AC03`.
- `invalid_creditor_financial_institution_identifier` - The destination routing
  number is invalid. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `RC04`.
- `end_customer_deceased` - The destination account holder is deceased.
  Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `MD07`.
- `narrative` - The reason is provided as narrative information in the
  additional information field.
- `transaction_forbidden` - Real-Time Payments transfers are not allowed to the
  destination account. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `AG01`.
- `transaction_type_not_supported` - Real-Time Payments transfers are not
  enabled for the destination account. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments
  reason code `AG03`.
- `unexpected_amount` - The amount of the transfer is different than expected by
  the recipient. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `AM09`.
- `amount_exceeds_bank_limits` - The amount is higher than the recipient is
  authorized to send or receive. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason
  code `AM14`.
- `invalid_creditor_address` - The creditor's address is required, but missing
  or invalid. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `BE04`.
- `unknown_end_customer` - The specified creditor is unknown. Corresponds to the
  Real-Time Payments reason code `BE06`.
- `invalid_debtor_address` - The debtor's address is required, but missing or
  invalid. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `BE07`.
- `timeout` - There was a timeout processing the transfer. Corresponds to the
  Real-Time Payments reason code `DS24`.
- `unsupported_message_for_recipient` - Real-Time Payments transfers are not
  enabled for the destination account. Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments
  reason code `NOAT`.
- `recipient_connection_not_available` - The destination financial institution
  is currently not connected to Real-Time Payments. Corresponds to the Real-Time
  Payments reason code `9912`.
- `real_time_payments_suspended` - Real-Time Payments is currently unavailable.
  Corresponds to the Real-Time Payments reason code `9948`.
- `instructed_agent_signed_off` - The destination financial institution is
  currently signed off of Real-Time Payments. Corresponds to the Real-Time
  Payments reason code `9910`.
- `processing_error` - The transfer was rejected due to an internal Increase
  issue. We have been notified.
- `other` - Some other error or issue has occurred.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_request_for_payment.Submission.payment_information_identification
documentation: The Real-Time Payments payment information identification of the request.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_request_for_payment.RealTimePaymentsRequestForPayment.id
documentation: The Real-Time Payments Request for Payment's identifier.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_request_for_payment.RealTimePaymentsRequestForPayment.amount
documentation: The transfer amount in USD cents.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_request_for_payment.RealTimePaymentsRequestForPayment.created_at
documentation:
The [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) date and time at which
the request for payment was created.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_request_for_payment.RealTimePaymentsRequestForPayment.currency
documentation:
The [ISO 4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) code for the transfer's
currency. For real-time payments transfers this is always equal to `USD`.

- `CAD` - Canadian Dollar (CAD)
- `CHF` - Swiss Franc (CHF)
- `EUR` - Euro (EUR)
- `GBP` - British Pound (GBP)
- `JPY` - Japanese Yen (JPY)
- `USD` - US Dollar (USD)

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_request_for_payment.RealTimePaymentsRequestForPayment.debtor_name
documentation: The name of the recipient the sender is requesting a transfer from.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_request_for_payment.RealTimePaymentsRequestForPayment.destination_account_number_id
documentation: The Account Number in which a successful transfer will arrive.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_request_for_payment.RealTimePaymentsRequestForPayment.expires_at
documentation:
The expiration time for this request, in UTC.

The requestee will not be able to pay after this date.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_request_for_payment.RealTimePaymentsRequestForPayment.fulfillment_transaction_id
documentation: The transaction that fulfilled this request.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_request_for_payment.RealTimePaymentsRequestForPayment.idempotency_key
documentation:
The idempotency key you chose for this object.

This value is unique across Increase and is used to ensure that a request is
only processed once. Learn more about
[idempotency](https://increase.com/documentation/idempotency-keys).

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_request_for_payment.RealTimePaymentsRequestForPayment.refusal
documentation:
If the request for payment is refused by the destination financial institution
or the receiving customer, this will contain supplemental details.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_request_for_payment.RealTimePaymentsRequestForPayment.rejection
documentation:
If the request for payment is rejected by Real-Time Payments or the destination
financial institution, this will contain supplemental details.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_request_for_payment.RealTimePaymentsRequestForPayment.remittance_information
documentation: Unstructured information that will show on the recipient's bank statement.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_request_for_payment.RealTimePaymentsRequestForPayment.source_account_number
documentation: The account number the request is sent to.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_request_for_payment.RealTimePaymentsRequestForPayment.source_routing_number
documentation: The receiver's American Bankers' Association (ABA) Routing Transit Number (RTN).

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_request_for_payment.RealTimePaymentsRequestForPayment.status
documentation:
The lifecycle status of the request for payment.

- `pending_submission` - The request for payment is queued to be submitted to
  Real-Time Payments.
- `pending_response` - The request for payment has been submitted and is pending
  a response from Real-Time Payments.
- `rejected` - The request for payment was rejected by the network or the
  recipient.
- `accepted` - The request for payment was accepted by the recipient but has not
  yet been paid.
- `refused` - The request for payment was refused by the recipient.
- `fulfilled` - The request for payment was fulfilled by the receiver.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_request_for_payment.RealTimePaymentsRequestForPayment.submission
documentation:
After the request for payment is submitted to Real-Time Payments, this will
contain supplemental details.

class_variables.name: increase.types.real_time_payments_request_for_payment.RealTimePaymentsRequestForPayment.type
documentation:
A constant representing the object's type.

For this resource it will always be `real_time_payments_request_for_payment`.

modules.name: increase.types.oauth_token_create_params
documentation: File generated from our OpenAPI spec by Stainless. See CONTRIBUTING.md for details.

class_variables.name: increase.types.oauth_token_create_params.OAuthTokenCreateParams.grant_type
documentation:
The credential you request in exchange for the code.

In Production, this is always `authorization_code`. In Sandbox, you can pass
either enum value.

- `authorization_code` - An OAuth authorization code.
- `production_token` - An OAuth production token.

class_variables.name: increase.types.oauth_token_create_params.OAuthTokenCreateParams.client_id
documentation: The public identifier for your application.

class_variables.name: increase.types.oauth_token_create_params.OAuthTokenCreateParams.client_secret
documentation:
The secret that confirms you own the application.

This is redundent given that the request is made with your API key but it's a
required component of OAuth 2.0.

class_variables.name: increase.types.oauth_token_create_params.OAuthTokenCreateParams.code
documentation:
The authorization code generated by the user and given to you as a query
parameter.

class_variables.name: increase.types.oauth_token_create_params.OAuthTokenCreateParams.production_token
documentation:
The production token you want to exchange for a sandbox token.

This is only available in Sandbox. Set `grant_type` to `production_token` to use
this parameter.

modules.name: increase.types.ach_transfer
